name.text: Finding the time taken and the total displacement of the elevator

questiontext.text:
An elevator in a tall office building starts from rest on the ground floor. It accelerates uniformly at \({@a_up@}\) m/s² until it reaches a cruising speed of \({@v_max@}\) m/s.

The elevator maintains this constant speed for \({@t_const@}\) seconds before decelerating uniformly at the same rate to stop at the top floor.

[[jsxgraph width="500px" height="350px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { boundingbox: [-1, 8, 15, -2], axis: true, showCopyright: false, defaultAxes: { x: { name: 'Time (s)', withLabel: true, label: { position: 'rt', offset: [-60, -30] } }, y: { name: 'Velocity (m/s)', withLabel: true, label: { position: 'rt', offset: [-20, -60], anchorX: 'middle', anchorY: 'middle', rotate: 90 } } } }); var v0 = {#v_max#}; var t1 = {#t_accel#}; var t2 = {#t_accel + t_const#}; var t3 = {#t_total#}; // Draw the V-T Graph board.create('segment', [[0, 0], [t1, v0]], {strokeColor: 'green', strokeWidth: 3, name: 'Accel'}); board.create('segment', [[t1, v0], [t2, v0]], {strokeColor: 'blue', strokeWidth: 3, name: 'Constant'}); board.create('segment', [[t2, v0], [t3, 0]], {strokeColor: 'red', strokeWidth: 3, name: 'Decel'}); // Labels // board.create('text', [-0.8, v0, v0 + " m/s"], {color: 'blue'}); [[/jsxgraph]]
  1. Calculate the time taken for the elevator to reach its cruising speed. [[input:ans1]] s [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]
  2. Calculate the total height (vertical displacement) of the building from the ground floor to the top floor. [[input:ans2]] m [[validation:ans2]] [[feedback:prt2]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

The elevator starts from rest \(u = 0\) and accelerates uniformly. We use the acceleration formula:

\(\begin{align} v &= u + at \\ {@v_max@} &= 0 + ({@a_up@} \times t_1) \\ t_1 &= \frac{{@v_max@}}{{@a_up@}} \\ t_1 &= {@ta1@} \text{ s} \end{align}\)

To find the total displacement (height) of the elevator, we calculate the area under each section of the Velocity-Time graph:

Step 1: Distance during Acceleration

The elevator accelerates from 0 to {@v_max@} m/s:

\(\begin{align} s_1 &= \frac{1}{2} \times \text{base} \times \text{height} \\ s_1 &= 0.5 \times {@ta1@} \times {@v_max@} \\ s_1 &= {@dist_accel@} \text{ m} \end{align}\)

Step 2: Distance at Constant Velocity

The elevator travels at a steady speed for {@t_const@} seconds:

\(\begin{align} s_2 &= \text{velocity} \times \text{time} \\ s_2 &= {@v_max@} \times {@t_const@} \\ s_2 &= {@dist_const@} \text{ m} \end{align}\)

Step 3: Total Displacement

Assuming symmetrical deceleration, the total height is the sum of all phases:

\(\begin{align} s_{total} &= s_{accel} + s_{const} + s_{decel} \\ s_{total} &= {@dist_accel@} + {@dist_const@} + {@dist_accel@} \\ s_{total} &= {@ta2@} \text{ m} \end{align}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
